strengths: Demonstrated experience with event-driven architectures and real-time systems in high-performance environments (e.g., Dunnhumby media, GISCollective)
Strong background in multi-language development and tooling for agentic workflows (e.g., MCP, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq)
Proven track record of scaling systems and contributing to open-source projects with a focus on maintainability and testing
concerns: Lacks direct experience in fintech infrastructure (e.g., credit systems, payment flows, or embedded finance)
Limited evidence of leadership in scaling engineering teams to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership layers
No explicit mention of regulatory compliance or auditability in AI-driven systems, a critical requirement for fintech
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong technical depth in event-driven architectures, real-time systems, and AI-agent tooling, which aligns well with the agentic AI requirements. However, his resume lacks direct fintech infrastructure experience and clear evidence of leading large-scale engineering teams or addressing regulatory compliance, which are critical for this role. His open-source contributions and tooling efforts suggest a focus on maintainability and testing, but his background does not fully match the fintech-specific demands of the position.
